original_abstract: This report examines language use trends since 1980 among the U.S. Hispanic population ages five and older in the fifty states and the District of Columbia, focusing on the languages spoken in Hispanic households and the share of Hispanics who are proficient in English. Accompanying this report are demographic and economic profiles of the U.S. Hispanic population, including trends for several characteristics since 1980.
humanized_abstract: Language Use Trends in the U.S. Hispanic Population This report examines language use trends among the U.S. Hispanic population ages five and older in the fifty states and the District of Columbia since 1980 in the languages spoken in Hispanic households and the language spoken with most others in the household as well as among Hispanics themselves in terms of their proficiency in English. We present demographic and economic profiles of the Hispanic population in the United States, including trends for selected characteristics since 1980.

We present demographic and economic profiles of the Hispanic population in the United States, including trends for selected characteristics since 1980.
